title: Rich Middle Eastern Mentaiko Tagine
description: A resinous and velvety Middle Eastern dish featuring mentaiko, this recipe is a quick-fix and paleo-friendly option for a weeknight dinner, simmered to perfection in a ceramic tagine.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in the ceramic tagine over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional 1 minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Stir in the ground cumin and smoked paprika and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the mentaiko, chicken broth, diced tomatoes, salt, and black pepper to the tagine.
Bring the mixture to a simmer, then reduce the heat to low and cook, covered, for 20 minutes.
Stir in the chopped fresh parsley and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with additional parsley if desired.
